# Air Quality and Smoke 
  1. [Home](https://deq.mt.gov/)
  2. [Air](https://deq.mt.gov/air/index)
  3. Programs
  4. Air Quality and Smoke


## Program Overview
The air quality in Montana is occasionally affected by smoke from wildfires during the summer months. Montana DEQ actively monitors smoke impacts from wildfires and provides regular smoke forecasts during the wildfire season.  
  
For those experiencing air quality affected by wildfire smoke, visit the Smoke Forecasts page or use the Visibility Guidelines to evaluate air quality in their area.
[Smoke Forecasts](https://deq.mt.gov/air/Programs/smokeforecasts)
[ Health Impacts from Smoke ](https://deq.mt.gov/air/Programs/airquality#accordion1-collapse1)
These recommendations for outdoor activities were developed by the Montana Department of Public Health and the Montana Department of Environmental Quality based on the amount of time spent outdoors and the state of air quality conditions, from good to hazardous.
[Outdoor Activity Guidelines: General Public](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Air/AirQuality/Documents/ActivityGuidelinesWildfireSmokeEventsGeneralPublic2025.pdf)
[Outdoor Activity Guidelines: Schools and Childcare Facilities](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Air/AirQuality/Documents/ActivityGuidelinesWildfireSmokeEventsSchools2025.pdf)
[ Visibility Guidelines ](https://deq.mt.gov/air/Programs/airquality#accordion1-collapse2)
The procedure for making personal observation to determine the forest fire smoke index value for local areas without National Weather Station (NWS) or Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) monitors is:
  1. Face away from the sun.
  2. Determine the limit of your visible range by looking for targets at known distances (miles).
  3. Visible range is that point at which even high contrast objects totally disappear.
  4. Use the values below to determine the local forest fire smoke category.


* * *
Health effects ratings based on visibility in miles 
Health Effects Categories |  Visibility Ranges (miles)  
---|---  
Very Unhealthy/Hazardous | Less than 2  
Unhealthy | 2-5  
Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups | 5-9  
Moderate | 9-13  
Good | 13+

# Mining
  1. [Home](https://deq.mt.gov/)
  2. [Mining](https://deq.mt.gov/mining/index)
  3. Permitting and Operator Assistance


For the information about Environmental Permits for all state agencies check out the _21st Edition of the_[**Montana Index of Environmental Permits**](http://leg.mt.gov/content/Publications/Environmental/2018-permit-index-final.pdf) guidebook published in 2018 by the Legislative Services Environmental Quality Council.
### Permitting and Operator Assistance
[ Coal Mining Forms ](https://deq.mt.gov/mining/assistance#accordion1-collapse1)
**A - Coal and Uranium Prospecting Forms**
  1. [Notice of Intent](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_NoticeofIntent.pdf)[](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/ProspectingTestPitForm.docx)
  2. [Prospecting Application Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_ProspectingApplication.pdf)

  4. [Revision Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/ProspectingRevisionForm.docx)
  5. [Renewal Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_ProspectingRenewal.pdf)
  6. [Transfer Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/ProspectingTransferForm.docx)
  7. [Assignment Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/ProspectingAssignmentForm.docx)
  8. [Monthly/Annual Report Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_ProspectingMonthlyReport.pdf)
  9. [Bond Release Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_ProspectingBondRelease.pdf)


**B - Surface Mine Permit Forms**
  1. [New Permit Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/SMPNewPermitForm.docx)
  2. [Amendment Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_SMPAmendment.pdf)
  3. [Major Revision Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_SMPMajorRevision.pdf)
  4. [Minor Revision Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_SMPMinorRevision.pdf)
  5. [Renewal Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_SMPRenewal.pdf)
  6. [Transfer Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/SMPTransferForm.docx)
  7. [Assignment Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/SMPAssignmentForm.docx)
  8. [Bond Release Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/InstructionsTemplate_SMPBondRelease.pdf)


**C - Bond Forms**
  1. SMP 
    1. Federal

      2. [Certificate of Deposit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Federal-CertificateofDepositAssignment.docx)
      3. [Irrevocable Letter of Credit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Federal-IrrevocableLetterofCredit.docx)
      4. [Surety Bond](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Federal-SuretyBond.docx)
      5. [Surety Rider](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Federal-SuretyRider.docx)
    2. Non-Federal 
      1. [Cash](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Non-Federal-CashBond.docx)
      2. [Certificate of Deposit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Non-Federal-CertificateofDepositAssignment.docx)
      3. [Irrevocable Letter of Credit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Non-Federal-IrrevocableLetterofCredit.docx)
      4. [Surety Bond](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Non-Federal-SuretyBond.docx)
      5. [Surety Rider](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Non-Federal-SuretyRider.docx)
    3. Tribal 
      1. [Cash](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Tribal-CashBond.docx)
      2. [Certificate of Deposit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Tribal-CertificateofDepositAssignment.docx)
      3. [Irrevocable Letter of Credit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Tribal-IrrevocableLetterofCredit.docx)
      4. [Surety Bond](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Tribal-SuretyBond.docx)
      5. [Surety Rider](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Tribal-SuretyRider.docx)
  2. Prospecting 
    1. [Cash Bond](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Prospecting-Cash-Bond.docx)
    2. [Certificate of Deposit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Prospecting-CertificateofDepositAssignment.docx)
    3. [Irrevocable Letter of Credit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Prospecting-LetterofCredit.docx)
    4. [Surety Bond](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Prospecting-SuretyBond.docx)
    5. [Surety Rider](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Bond-Forms/Prospecting-SuretyRider.docx)


**D - Blasting Forms**
  1. [Blaster Certification/Renewal Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/BlasterCertificationForm.docx)
  2. [New Blaster Affidavit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/BlasterNewAffidavit.docx)
  3. [Renewal Blaster Affidavit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/BlasterRecertificationAffidavit.docx)
  4. [Blaster Training Manual](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/BlasterTrainingManual.pdf)
    1. [ISEE Blasters Handbook](https://isee.org/resources/publications/the-blasters-handbook)
    2. [Austin Powder Company: The Complete Blasters Guide](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/AustinPowderTheCompleteBlastersGuide.pdf)
  5. Online Blasting Classes Approved for Montana: 
    1. [ISEE Training Webpage](https://isee.org/training)
    2. [Wyoming Blasters Webpage](https://deq.wyoming.gov/land-quality/blasting/)
    3. [OSMRE Blasters Webpage](https://www.osmre.gov/programs/regulating-active-coal-mines/blasting)


**E - Other Forms**
  1. [Map Affidavit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/Map_Affidavit.docx)
  2. [Designation of Mine Contacts](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/DesignationOfMineContacts.docx)
  3. [Designation of Permit Coordinator](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/DesignationOfPermitCoordinator.docx)


**F - Guidelines (updates coming soon)**
**G - Reference Documents**
  1. U.S.D.A. Soil Conservation Service,[1951 Soil Survey Manual ](https://www.nrcs.usda.gov/resources/guides-and-instructions/soil-survey-manual)- ARM 17.24.301(114)
  2. U.S.D.A Soil Conservation Service, [National Soils Handbook](http://soils.usda.gov/technical/handbook/) - ARM 17.24.301(115)
  3. U.S.D.A. Soil Conservation Service, [Soil Taxonomy (Handbook 436)](https://www.nrcs.usda.gov/resources/guides-and-instructions/soil-taxonomy) - ARM 17.24.301(115)
  4. U.S.D.A. Soil Conservation Service, [Technical Release No. 60 (Earth Dams and Reservoirs)](https://directives.nrcs.usda.gov/sites/default/files2/1712940996/28860.pdf) - ARM 17.24.642(1)(f)
  5. U.S.D.A. Soil Conservation Service, [Pond 378](https://www.nrcs.usda.gov/resources/guides-and-instructions/pond-no-378-conservation-practice-standard) - ARM 17.24.642(1)(f)
  6. 40 CFR Part 136, [Guidelines Establishing Test Procedures for the Analysis of Pollutants](https://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?SID=afd174646b95e6d91653bae49921ad69&mc=true&node=pt40.25.136&rgn=div5) - ARM 17.24.645(6)
  7. Montana DEQ, Montana Numeric Water Quality Standards, [Circular WQB-7](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Water/WQPB/Standards/PDF/DEQ7/DEQ-7.pdf)- ARM 17.24.645(6)
  8. 40 CFR Part 434, [Coal Mining Point Source Category BPT, PAT, PCT Limitations and New Source Performance Standards](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/CFR-2002-title40-vol26/pdf/CFR-2002-title40-vol26-part434.pdf) - ARM 17.24.646(6)
  9. U.S.D.A. Soil Conservation Service, Land Capability Classification for Montana - ARM 17.24.746
  10. Maas and Hoffman, [Crop Salt Tolerance-Current Assessment](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/MaasHoffman_1977.pdf) - ARM 17.24.806(1)
  11. [30 CFR 761.16](http://www.gpo.gov/fdsys/pkg/CFR-2003-title30-vol3/pdf/CFR-2003-title30-vol3-sec761-16.pdf) - ARM 17.24.1133(3)[](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/CoalUranium/Forms/PublicNoticeExamples/PublicNoticeExample_BondRelease.docx)


[ Hard Rock Mining Forms ](https://deq.mt.gov/mining/assistance#accordion1-collapse2)
**A - Operating Permit -[Fee Schedule](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/Fee%20Schedule.pdf)**
  1. Rock Products Application Packet [Zip](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/RockProductsApplicationPacket/Rock%20Products%20Application%20Packet.zip)  
 _For New Permit and Major Amendment applications for Rock Product operating permits. See Rock Products Application Cover letter for requirements for a rock products operating permit._

    6. [Major Amendment Flow Chart](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/RockProductsApplicationPacket/Major%20Amendment%20Flow%20Chart_Rock%20Products.pdf)
    7. [Hard Rock & Placer Mining Requirements](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/RockProductsApplicationPacket/Hard%20Rock%20&%20Placer%20Mining%20Requirements.pdf)
    8. [Additional Approvals](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/RockProductsApplicationPacket/Additional%20Approvals.pdf)
  2. Hard Rock (Non Rock Products) Application Packet [Zip](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/HardRockMiningNonRockProductsApplicationPacket.zip)  
 _For New Permit and Major Amendment applications for operations that do not meet the requirements for a Rock Products operating permit._

    4. [Operating Permit Application Compliance Certification](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/Operating%20Permit%20Application%20Compliance%20Certification.docx)
    5. [Permit Application Flow Chart](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/Permit%20Application%20Flow%20Chart.pdf)
    6. [Major Amendment Flow Chart](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/Major%20Amendment%20Flow%20Chart.pdf)
    7. [Hard Rock & Placer Mining Requirements](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/Hard%20Rock%20&%20Placer%20Mining%20Requirements.pdf)
    8. [Additional Approvals](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/NonRockProductsApplicationPacket/Additional%20Approvals.pdf)
  3. Revision Forms 
    1. [Revision Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/MinorRevisionForms/Revision%20Form.docx)
    2. [Example Revision Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/MinorRevisionForms/Example%20Revision%20Application.pdf)
  4. [Minor Amendment Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/Minor%20Amendment%20Form.docx)
  5. Annual Report Forms  

    1. [Annual Report Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/AnnualReportForm/Annual%20Report%20Form.pdf)
    2. [Spreadsheet for Multi-Site Permits](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/AnnualReportFormsMulti-sitePermits/Spreadsheet%20for%20Multi%20Site%20Permits.xlsx)
  6. Annual Fee Online Payment Instructions 
    1. [Electronic Operating Permit Fee Payment](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/Electronic%20Operating%20Permit%20Fee%20Payment.pdf)
  7. [Bond Release Request Form](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/OP/Bond%20Release%20Request%20Form.docx)

  

**B - Exploration Licenses[Fee Schedule (_Effective July 1, 2025_)](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/SMES/SMESExploLicenseFees.pdf)**

**D - Bond Forms**
Please contact the Permit License Technician at 444-4953 for the bond instrument forms.
  1. Reclamation Bond Requirements - [PDF](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/Hardrock/Forms/BondReqHandout.pdf)


**E - Other Government Requirements/Guidelines**
Additional guidelines or permit requirements associated with hard rock mining from other government entities
  1. [Sage Grouse Advisory](https://sagegrouse.mt.gov/Team)
  2. Exploration and Mining on State Lands/School Trust - [Department of Natural Resources Trust Lands Management](http://dnrc.mt.gov/divisions/trust)
  3. Mining Claims on Federal Land - [Bureau of Land Management Montana/Dakotas](https://www.blm.gov/montana-dakotas)

[ Opencut Mining Forms ](https://deq.mt.gov/mining/assistance#accordion1-collapse3)
**Request for Pre-Application Meeting**
DEQ strongly recommends Operators request a meeting before submitting an application. In response, an Opencut scientist will contact the Operator and schedule a visit to provide guidance on the application process and recommendations on the proposed Opencut operation. Activities within or adjacent to [designated sage grouse habitats](https://sagegrouse.mt.gov/projects/) require consultation with the [Montana Sage Grouse Habitat Conservation Program](https://sagegrouse.mt.gov/). The DEQ Sage Grouse Advisory is also a source of information.
  1. [Request for Pre-Application Meeting](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-RequestforPre-ApplicationMeeting.docx)


**A - Instructional Documents**
These guidance documents may provide additional assistance for permitting activities:
  1. [Which Permit do I Need?](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2023-Which%20Opencut%20Permit%20Application%20do%20I%20Need%20How%20To.pdf)
  2. [Electronic Submittal of Opencut Documents](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2023-Electronic%20Submittal%20of%20OC%20Documents.docx)
  3. [Electronic Application Fee Payment Instructions](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2024-Application%20Fee%20Electronic%20Payment%20Instructions.pdf)


**B - Permit and Amendment Applications**
Use the following documents to apply for an Opencut Mining Permit or Amendment:
  1. [Standard Opencut Mining Permit Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Permit-StandardOpencutMiningApplication.docx)
  2. [Dryland Opencut Mining Permit Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Permit-DrylandOpencutPermit.docx)
  3. [Landowner Consultation with Attachment A](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-LandownerConsultation%20with%20Attachment%20A%202025-11-20%20Update.docx)
  4. [Zoning Compliance](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-ZoningCompliance.docx)
  5. [Operator Certification of Surface Landowners and Occupied Dwelling Units for a Standard Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Operator%20Certification%20of%20Surface%20Landownersand%20Occupied%20Dwelling%20Units.xlsx)
  6. [Boundary Coordinate Table (BCT)](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-BoundaryCoordinateTable.xlsx)
  7. [Reclamation Bond Spreadsheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-ReclamationBondSpreadsheet.xlsx)
  8. [Weed Board Notification of Opencut Operation](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-WeedBoardNotificationofOpencutOperation.docx)
  9. [SHPO File Search Request Form for Opencut Site](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2024-SHPOFileSearchRequestforOpencutWebsite.xlsx)


**C - Bond Forms**
The most updated bonds are dated 2/26. As of March 1, 2026, Opencut will require all new bonds and riders to be on the new forms and will not be accepting older versions.
One of the following bonding instruments is also required:
  1. [Cash Bond for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-Cash.docx "Cash Bond for Opencut Mining Permit")
  2. [Certificate of Deposit Assignment](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-CertificateofDepositAssignment.docx)
  3. [Irrevocable Letter Of Credit for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-IrrevocableLetterofCredit.docx)
  4. [Property Bond for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-Property.docx)
  5. [Surety Bond for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-Surety.docx)
  6. [Surety Bond Rider for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-SuretyRider.docx)


For Bentonite Operators ONLY:
  1. [Joint Surety Bond for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-JointSurety.docx)
  2. [Joint Surety Bond Rider for Opencut Mining Permit](https://deq.mt.gov/Files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Bond-JointSuretyRider.docx)


**D - Liability or Bond Release Request Form**
Upon Completion of Reclamation, use the following form to request Liability **or** Bond Release:
  1. [Phase I Release Request](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-PhaseIReleaseRequest.docx)
  2. [Phase II Release Request](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-PhaseIIReleaseRequest.docx)


**E - Other Applications**
Use the following documents for various permitting functions as appropriate:
  1. [Limited Opencut Operation (LOO)](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-LimitedOpencutOperation.docx)
  2. [5 Year Reclamation Only Amendment](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-5-YearRecOnlyAmendment.docx?ver=2021-05-17-103713-147)
  3. [Application to Change Reclamation Date](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-ApplicationtoChangeReclamationDate.docx)
  4. [Change Postmining Land Use Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-PostminingLandUseChangeApplication.docx)
  5. [Request to Modify Bonded Acreage](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-RequesttoModifyBondedAcreage.docx)
  6. [Assignment of Opencut Mining Permit Application](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-AssignmentofOpencutMiningPermitApplication.docx)


**F - Other Forms**
  1. [Decline Public Meeting](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-DeclinePublicMeeting.docx)


**G - Guidelines**
The following documents provide technical guidance for Opencut permitting process:
  1. [Map Guideline](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Guideline-Map.pdf)
  2. [Seed Mix Guideline](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Guideline-SeedMix.pdf)
  3. [Soil Guideline](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Guideline-Soil.pdf)
  4. [Wash Plant Settling Pond Guideline](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Guideline-WashPlantSettlingPond.pdf)


**H - Worksheets**
Use these worksheets to help you in the Opencut permitting process:
  1. [Standard Determining Depth to Groundwater Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-Standard-DeterminingDepthtoGroundwater.docx)
  2. [Dryland Determining Depth to Groundwater Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-Dryland-DeterminingDepthtoGroundwater.docx)[](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2024-Worksheet-DeterminingDepthtoGroundwater.docx)
  3. [Pond and Wetland Design Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-PondandWetlandDesign.docx)
  4. [Standard Stream/Waterway Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-Stream-Waterway-Standard.docx)
  5. [Dryland Stream/Waterway Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-Stream-Waterway-Dryland.docx)
  6. [Soil & Overburden Quantity & Replacement Depth Calculation Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2026-Worksheet-SoilOverburdenQuantity-ReplacementDepth-Calculation.docx)


**I - Additional Opencut Information**
These resources provide additional help to you in the Opencut permitting process:
  1. [Public Notice and Public Meeting Requirements](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2022-PublicNoticePublicMeetingRequirements.pdf)
  2. [Opencut Public Participation Flyer](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2022-Opencut%20Public%20Participation%20Flyer.pdf)
  3. [DEQ Opencut Frequently Asked Questions](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2021-DEQ%20OPENCUT%20FREQUENTLY%20ASKED%20QUESTIONS.pdf)
  4. [DEQ Air Quality Bureau Frequently Asked Questions](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2021-DEQAIRFAQ.pdf)
  5. [Water Protection Bureau Permitting Guide](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2021-Water%20Protection%20Bureau%20Permitting%20Guide.pdf)
  6. [DNRC Opencut Mining: Do You Need a Water Right?](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2021-DNRC-Opencut%20Mining%20Do%20you%20Need%20a%20Water%20Right.pdf?ver=2021-05-17-091506-543)
  7. [BLM Mineral Ownership Pamphlet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/BLM_Mineral_Ownership.pdf)[](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/2021-Water%20Protection%20Bureau%20Permitting%20Guide.pdf)[](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Land/OpenCut/Forms/SHPO-OperatorProcess.pdf)

# Laws and Regulations
  1. [Home](https://deq.mt.gov/)
  2. [About](https://deq.mt.gov/about/index)
  3. Laws and Regulations


[ Administrative Rules of Montana ](https://deq.mt.gov/about/lawsandregs#accordion1-collapse1)
DEQ is responsible for administering and enforcing several chapters of rules within Title 17 of the Administrative Rules of Montana (ARM). The links below to the specific chapters on the Montana Secretary of State website are provided as a convenience for interested parties. Administrative rules are periodically reviewed and revised by DEQ and some lapse in time may occur between adoption and the electronic posting of new or revised rules. Electronic rules on the Secretary of State website are updated quarterly and may not reflect the most current version adopted. For an official version of the Administrative Rules of Montana, please contact the Secretary of State at (406) 444-2055.
#### Table of Contents (Title 17)
  * [Chapter 1](https://rules.mt.gov/browse/collections/aec52c46-128e-4279-9068-8af5d5432d74/sections/ac014a3e-f122-41c5-b8d8-8f712277809c) - Organization of Department

[ Montana Code Annotated ](https://deq.mt.gov/about/lawsandregs#accordion1-collapse2)
The laws in the Montana Code Annotated are passed by the Montana State Legislature, which consists of the Montana House of Representatives and the Montana Senate. DEQ is charged with protecting a clean and healthy environment as guaranteed to our citizens by our State Constitution in Titles 75, 76, and 82, Montana Code Annotated.

[ Final Rule Adoption and Amendment Notices ](https://deq.mt.gov/about/lawsandregs#accordion1-collapse3)
In the table below you will find information on DEQ rulemaking for the past two years. Each archived rule notice includes a link to official public notices on the Montana Secretary of State's website. If you are looking for older information, please contact Loryn Johnson 406-444-1388.

Effective Date | Purpose & Link to Online Rule Notices

[ Litigation Accountability ](https://deq.mt.gov/about/lawsandregs#accordion1-collapse4)
DEQ is committed to maintaining transparency regarding litigation activities.
To fulfill our commitment to transparency and accountability, DEQ provides litigation updates on this page, including:
  * Cases where DEQ prevailed in court
  * Case trends
  * Cases where DEQ incurred attorney fees/costs


_For complete court filings, opinions, and orders, please contact the respective County District Court or Montana Supreme Court._

# Grant, Loan and Rebate Programs
  1. [Home](https://deq.mt.gov/)
  2. [Energy](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/index)
  3. Grant, Loan and Rebate Programs


## Program Overview
DEQ currently offers grants and loan programs for alternative energy systems, alternative fuel transportation, and electric vehicle infrastructure. Additional tax credits and rebates recently passed by congress will roll out soon. Some of those grants and rebates will be administered by DEQ, while others will be administered federally. 
Find more information on DEQ’s current programs by selecting alternative fuels and transportation or alternative energy loans below.
#### Programs and Funding Opportunities from DEQ
Congress enacted the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL) and Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) providing once-in-a-generation investments into our nation’s infrastructure, manufacturing competitiveness, domestic energy production, and clean energy deployment. Numerous funding opportunities are available through these laws including grants, loans, tax credits, and rebates. DEQ’s Energy Bureau is implementing programs allocated to state energy offices, while other programs are implemented federally or through tax credits.   
  
Programs are at varying stages of development within Federal agencies. Timelines for implementation vary by program. As guidance is developed, the Energy Bureau will provide updates for programs implemented by DEQ. This includes grid resiliency projects, Energy Efficiency and Conservation Block Grants, energy efficiency loans, electric vehicle charging infrastructure, low emission school bus grants, workforce development grants, building code development, and energy efficiency and electrification rebates.
### New Funding Opportunity
[ Energy Efficiency Conservation Block Grant (EECBG) Request for Application ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/incentive#accordion1-collapse1)
The Montana Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) Energy Bureau is issuing a Request for Applications (RFA) for Montana public K–12 schools and local government entities that have not previously received a direct Energy Efficiency and Conservation Block Grant (EECBG). The process includes two application phases: first, eligible applicants must submit a Draft Application (DA) through email to Bonnie Rouse no later than **10:00 PM MST on Sunday, February 8, 2026**. After submitting the DA, applicants must complete a Formal Application (FA) through the state’s online system, eMACS, by **2:00 PM MST on Friday, March 6, 2026**. DEQ technical assistance is available to applicants during the FA development phase.
This funding opportunity is supported through the State of Montana’s participation in the U.S. Department of Energy’s Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) EECBG Program (Grant No. DE‑EE0000356) and follows all DOE requirements. DEQ has approximately $480,000 available for this round of funding, and each applicant may request up to $100,000. Awards will be reimbursed in phases as project tasks are completed and no cost share is required.
  * [Webinar Recording from Jan 28th 2026](https://youtu.be/DKeHUGk0N8s?si=JFJRbfEnABIFIHKp)
  * [Jan 28th 2026 Webinar Power Point](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Energy/Documents/EECBG-Webinar_Final.pdf)
  * [Sample Calculation Worksheet](https://deq.mt.gov/files/Energy/Documents/EECGB_Sample_Calculation.xlsx)


#### FINANCIAL AND INCENTIVE CONTACTS
Program Support Specialist   
Kristyn Fullerton (406) 444-6460

### DEQ Programs
##### [ Alternative Fuels & Transportation  _Electric Vehicle Infrastructure, Alternative Fuels, Zero-Emission Bus Programs_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/fuels)
##### [ Alternative Energy Loans  _Low-interest loans for alternativeenergy & energy savings_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/AERLP)
##### [ Energy Efficiency & Conservation Block Grant  _Assistance for local governments_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/conservation)
##### [ Grid Resiliency and Reliability  _Grant funding for electrical grid upgrades and resiliency_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/grid)
##### [ Home Energy Rebates Program  _Inflation Reduction Act rebate programs_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/efficiency)
##### [ Multifamily Energy Retrofit Loan Program  _Low-interest loans for energy efficiency improvements in affordable housing facilities_ ](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/MERL)
### Additional Federal Programs and Opportunities
#### Tax Incentives 
Tax credits are available for homeowners, commercial small businesses, local governments, Tribal governments, schools and Universities, utilities, electric cooperatives, and project developers.
Opportunities are available for energy efficiency projects, renewable energy and power generation, electric vehicles and charging infrastructure, manufacturing, transmission, project sitting, and reinvestment financing.
[Learn More](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/tax-credits)
* * *
#### Funding through Other Agencies 
Additional funding is available through the U.S. Department of Energy, U.S. Department of Agriculture, U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, and Montana Department of Health and Human Services.
Opportunities are available for homeowners, commercial small businesses, local governments, Tribal governments, schools and Universities, utilities, electric cooperatives, and project developers
[Learn More](https://deq.mt.gov/energy/Programs/other-funding)
